rationale.R1.2: The Results and Discussion sections contain a load-bearing logical inconsistency: ECE is an error metric (lower = better), so CALIGRA's ECE of 0.094 vs. the baseline's 0.058 on RefCOCOg-test means CALIGRA is worse, not better — yet the text asserts 'substantially improved calibration' and claims confirmation of temperature-scaling theory. This contradiction is critical under any substantive interpretation of the delegation.
rationale.R1.3: Every quantitative claim in the Results section (both RefCOCOg-test and CALIGRA-OOD splits) is asserted as evidence of improvement, but the reported numbers show the opposite direction; evidence-claim alignment is therefore directly implicated regardless of whether the requested help is review or polish.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' is the canonical phrasing for writing/editing assistance, making prose quality a plausible primary load-bearing responsibility under one reading of the delegation.
